title: pi-lens ast-grep rule
description: A single ast-grep rule loaded by pi-lens. Place files under rules/ast-grep-rules/rules/. Supports multiple documents per file separated by ---.

pattern.description: Ast-grep pattern syntax. Avoid single bare metavariables like '$VAR' (too broad, filtered out).

regex.description: Regular expression matched against the node's text.

has.description: The node must have a descendant matching this condition.

not.description: Negation — the node must NOT match this condition.

any.description: OR — matches if any item matches. Minimum 1 item.

all.description: AND — matches only if all items match. Minimum 1 item.

inside.description: Ancestor must match. Combined with `stopBy: end` it searches ALL ancestors (default `stopBy: neighbor` = direct parent).

stopBy.description: How `inside`/`has`/`follows`/`precedes` walk the tree. `neighbor` (default) = direct parent/child/sibling; `end` = walk all the way to the root / leaves.

field.description: Field name constraint — e.g. `field: name` on an `import_specifier` matches the import's local binding.

nthChild.description: Match only the Nth child of its parent. Used to disambiguate repeated siblings (e.g. the first vs second argument in a call).

id.description: Unique rule identifier. A project rule with the same id as a built-in overrides the built-in.

note.description: Extended guidance shown in the detail view. Markdown supported. Supports block scalar (|).

rule.description: The matching condition. Must contain at least one of: pattern, kind, regex, has, any, all, not.

constraints.description: Metavariable regex constraints — `KEY: { regex: "..." }` narrows what `$KEY` will match. Supported by the napi engine (and the ast-grep CLI/LSP).
